author: The Daily GOOD

title: Zombies, audacity, and love
description: Hello GOOD people. Welcome aboard today’s body tour: zombified frog cells (yes, really), ocean-crossing Stone Agers, and the surprisingly sweet science of love. We’re diving into guts, grit, and gooey feelings.
author: The Daily GOOD

title: Technology saves nature, DNA dogs and Iggy Pop makes history
description: Mother Nature has a friend in technology, a heartwarming dog reunion and watch the birth of crowd surfing in real time.
author: The Daily GOOD

title: Bye bye Boomer habits, TRON meets Playboy and why our brains love TV reruns
description: When politeness hits its expiration date, the wild TRON/Playboy collaboration, and why our brains think watching the same shows over and over is a GOOD time.
author: The Daily GOOD

title: The most affordable house in America, AI takes over HR (and fails), and why smart people just don't care
description: Solving America's housing crisis doesn't have to be spartan, an AI job rejection letter that's so cringe you have to laugh and why intelligent people have less empathy. We've got some GOOD solutions, GOOD laughs, and GOOD science for you today.
author: The Daily GOOD

title: Unpacking Amazon 'mystery boxes,' the science of cool and Bob Dylan, sitcom star
description: Expensive and baffling Amazon 'mystery boxes' have invaded thrift stores and there's not much Goodwill to be had, it turns out being cool is a six part science and remembering the time Bob Dylan decided to show up and jam on a forgettable 90's sitcom. Don't worry Bob, it's all GOOD.
author: The Daily GOOD

title: Zeros all day
description: Zero clothes, zero dollars, zero frills. It's Wednesday's GOOD stuff, and for being three stories of minimalism, there's a lot to love.
author: The Daily GOOD

title: First times, last words, and 96 lonely dollars
description: Today we’re zooming from first times (👀), to last words (💔), to a Gen Z budget that leaves exactly $96 rattling around an empty wallet (💸).
author: The Daily GOOD

title: A.I. can't wear socks
description: If, for some reason, you want to pay college tuition so an A.I. can do all the learning, we've got bad news for you. But if your feet get cold at night, we've got great news! And if you're a writer that wants to change the course of human history with a single article, we've got inspiration.
author: The Daily GOOD

title: GOOD Vibrations for your weekend
description: As we head into a complicated weekend, we remember a music legend, celebrate fathers, and get a peek at what the world might look like without them.
author: The Daily GOOD

title: Hue knew?
description: Yellow becomes defiance. Green becomes love. And “orange” didn’t even exist for ancient English speakers. Let’s explore some shades of color and meaning.
author: The Daily GOOD

title: Gas, fumes, and hot air
description: Farts that might save your heart, fumes that nearly stole a life, and some top-shelf grandpa wisdom that could clear your mental fog.
author: The Daily GOOD

title: 8,200 calories a day...
description: We've got a story about the rarest Costco find ever, the smart characters we love, and a president who single-handedly supported the beef industry with his fork.
author: The Daily GOOD

title: Pee Wee Herman crashes the BBQ
description: We've got two stories of celebrity run-ins and 4 words that might help make you less awkward if one ever happens to you.
author: The Daily GOOD

title: Your Monday survival kit: Animal bloopers, social hacks, and driving dogs
description: What do you get when you cross wildlife photography, behavioral science, and a car-steering canine? Just the kind of newsletter that makes Monday bearable.
author: The Daily GOOD

title: Feel-good fuel for your Friday: NASA houseplants, heroic exits, and a wild iPhone hack
description: This weekend’s GOOD stuff: Quitting like a boss, scrolling through 1582, and going full NASA at Home Depot
author: The Daily GOOD

title: Woo, woof, wow: Science goes full party trick today
description: Your dance moves might attract a mate. Dogs can smell liars. And the Earth? Apparently, it has a resting heartbeat. Welcome to your new favorite trivia trio.
author: The Daily GOOD
